channel.title: GardenDesign on Nature Works
channel.description: Recent content in GardenDesign on Nature Works

item.title: Drone
item.description:
The first test flight of the new drone! It didn’t crash.

I bought a drone for photos of garden plans. Originally I thought a drone would only be useful for large gardens but I really think it’ll be an invaluable way of documenting planting plans. http://localhost:1313/blog/240118-drone/

item.title: On cow paths & desire lines
item.description:
Years ago, I was a web designer, and where people wanted to go was a key idea in web design. The same applies to garden design.

I took a trip to the health centre today, for a 10 tonnes of hardcore shoulder injury I sustained a couple of years ago. http://localhost:1313/blog/231220-cow-paths/

item.title: Rejection
item.description:
I had an email back from a prospective client, and this is what it said

I tried to get some work via Bark.com, for a local builder who wanted a garden designing so he could sell the house. http://localhost:1313/blog/230403-rejection/

item.title: The forest garden style
item.description:
What does a forest garden even look like?

I phoned up a potential client today, a local builder who wants a garden that looks nice so he can sell the house. http://localhost:1313/blog/230401-forest-garden-style/

item.title: CAD for Gardeners
item.description:
The end of the beginning as I publish my new online class

I was going to write about a permaculture musings post I read, which talks about design and colonialism. http://localhost:1313/blog/230119-end-begin/

item.title: It’s a wrap
item.description:
I have finished filming the CAD for Gardeners online class!

Long day filming and making finishing touches to my CAD for Gardeners online course. http://localhost:1313/blog/230110-wrap/

item.title: GIS
item.description: GIS stands for Geographic Information System, software that can analyse data that has a geographic component. http://localhost:1313/blog/230107-gis/

item.title: Designing a small forest garden
item.description:
A small garden can be a forest garden, and this is a peek into how I approach a small forest garden design.

I really think that your small garden can be a forest garden, because to be a forest garden is more abut the principles than the size. http://localhost:1313/blog/designing-small-forest-garden/
